title: Fig. 2 in First report of Enterocytozoon bieneusi and Cryptosporidium spp. in peafowl (Pavo cristatus) in China
author: Feng, Sheng-Yong
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 2. Phylogenetic analysis of Cryptosporidium spp. using Neighbor-Joining (NJ) method based on sequences of the small subunit ribosomal RNA (SSU rRNA) gene. Bootstrap values> 50% are shown (1000 replicates). Isolates obtained in the present study are indicated by solid square. The SSU rRNA gene sequence of Eimeria faurei is used as the outgroup.
steps_raw: Fig. 2. Phylogenetic analysis of Cryptosporidium spp. using Neighbor-Joining (NJ) method based on sequences of the small subunit ribosomal RNA (SSU rRNA) gene. Bootstrap values> 50% are shown (1000 replicates). Isolates obtained in the present study are indicated by solid square. The SSU rRNA gene sequence of Eimeria faurei is used as the outgroup.
